status: revised DC rove plan after late-arrival C&O Maryland opener
utcDay: Sunday UTC day begins Saturday 2026-06-20 at 20:00 EDT; the late-arrival Maryland opener is Chesapeake and Ohio Canal NHP at Anglers because all-states credit is higher priority than the Greenbelt/BW Parkway 2-fer. Count C&O as US-0015, then use LBJ and the expanded Mall route to reach 20 before the Sunday UTC rollover if pace holds.

sources.label: CWops Solid Copy No. 193, February 2026, W8WZ “Activating DC”
sources.notes: Article describes the LBJ 7-fer, the 4-fer fallback at Columbia Island Marina/picnic tables, and the 15-park Cheetah route completed on 2025-12-22.

sources.label: GeoJSON boundary and trail checks
sources.notes: Fresh NPS ArcGIS/OSM GeoJSON files were cached for Greenbelt, National Capital Parks, Baltimore-Washington Parkway, and Star-Spangled Banner NHT. Sweetgum is inside Greenbelt, outside the NACA and BW Parkway polygons, and about 774 m / 2,540 ft from the official STSP centerline.

sources.label: Imported n-fer evidence from top activators
NPS Fort McHenry operating hours
sources.notes: Official NPS page updated 2026-05-20 says summer weekend grounds and parking lot hours end at 18:00, gates close then, and last weekend entry is 17:45.

sources.label: NPS LBJ Memorial Grove operating hours
sources.notes: Official NPS page says LBJ Memorial Grove is open year round from 06:00 to 22:00 and Columbia Island Marina is open 06:00 to midnight.

sources.label: Greenbelt Park Sweetgum and campground access
sources.notes: NPS material says Greenbelt Park is dawn-to-dusk and campground 24/7 access is framed around campers. Without a campsite, do not use Greenbelt as the late-night all-states Maryland anchor.

sources.label: C&O Canal current conditions and Anglers access
sources.notes: Current NPS conditions list the Anglers/Carderock/Great Falls corridor access points and parking as open; Anglers is the cleaner late-arrival Maryland fallback for US-0015.

sources.label: Local activator email feedback, June 2026
sources.notes: KB3WAV corroborated Fort McHenry gate closure; W3LAM corroborated LBJ vehicle access and seven-reference right-side parking-area operation; N2NWK suggested Greenbelt as a four-reference MD/DC-area option.

countingNotes: Count unique POTA entities on the Sunday UTC day, not local calendar day.

For the late-arrival all-states plan, Chesapeake and Ohio Canal NHP at Anglers is the primary Maryland opener. Count US-0015 as the clean Maryland entity; Greenbelt/BW Parkway remains a higher-count fallback only with legal access and a defensible exact position.
Fort McHenry is not usable at/after 20:00 EDT: official NPS summer weekend gate closure is 18:00. Treat it as a pre-18:00 optional Saturday-local stop only.

POTA multi-loc activations require the activator and all station equipment to be inside the overlapping valid area for every declared reference; trail references require public land within 30.5 m / 100 ft of the trail.

The LBJ 7-fer is corroborated by W8WZ article evidence and local W3LAM feedback: the right side of the parking area / LBJ operating area is inside US-0784, US-0670, US-4564, US-4567, US-4581, US-9901, and US-9898.

With C&O instead of the Greenbelt 2-fer, the expanded Mall route needs Jefferson, President’s Park, and WWI to reach 20 Sunday-UTC entities; Meadowood remains the practical Virginia state finish even if it spills past 20:00 EDT.

timelineAssumptions.notes: Timeline is a planning model, not a schedule commitment. Use 45 minutes per activation stop for planning, with 30 minutes as the absolute floor if the day needs to compress. The C&O opener banks Maryland for all-states; the expanded Mall route supplies the Sunday-UTC count.

primaryTimeline.movementFromPrior: Late Saturday route arrival from Bear, DE / Fox Point corridor; use Anglers parking for a clean Maryland C&O Canal activation
primaryTimeline.timelineNote: All-states Maryland anchor. Count US-0015 only; this replaces the Greenbelt/BW Parkway 2-fer because there is no Greenbelt campsite and legal late access matters more than the extra entity.

primaryTimeline.movementFromPrior: Foggy Bottom hotel, or count this earlier Saturday night from the Columbia Island Marina side if timing still works
primaryTimeline.timelineNote: Jackpot after the C&O Maryland opener. Adds seven unique references; local feedback says this can be vehicle/base-station friendly from the right side of the marina/LBJ parking area.
Starts the expanded Mall walk from the south/east side. Adds Jefferson plus National Mall if National Mall has not already been counted.
Adds FDR; National Mall is already duplicate after Jefferson.
Short Mall walk; National Mall is already duplicate after Jefferson.
Adds Lincoln; this is still before the Cheetah threshold in the C&O opener route.
Cheetah threshold with the C&O opener and LBJ 7-fer.
End of the W8WZ article-backed core sequence. In the C&O opener route this gets the count to 18, so President’s Park and WWI are still needed for 20.
Expanded Mall/downtown add-on required after replacing Greenbelt with C&O. Use the south/Ellipse side when that is the more defensible public operating area.
Reaches 20 unique references in the C&O opener route if all prior stops hold. Ford’s Theatre is the first practical downtown buffer beyond this.

primaryTimeline.label: Return to Foggy Bottom hotel / get car
primaryTimeline.timelineNote: Return to the hotel to pick up the car. Walking back is about an hour; use rideshare if protecting the Virginia state finish.
Practical Virginia all-states finish. Local advice says Meadowood’s Mustang Equestrian Trailhead is the best reliable radio spot, with free parking and a straightforward car-adjacent setup.

primaryTimeline.label: Fredericksburg hotel
primaryTimeline.timelineNote: Booked Sunday night anchor after the rove.

optionalTimelineAddOns.placement: Use instead of Meadowood only if the day is running well enough for the extra complexity.
optionalTimelineAddOns.timelineImpact: Replaces Meadowood with about 18 more driving minutes and one more entity, reaching 20 if the 2-fer verifies.
optionalTimelineAddOns.timelineNote: Mason Neck/Hartwell are adjacent but separate parks, Hartwell has stricter setup limits, and Mason Neck State Park adds an entry fee.

optionalTimelineAddOns.placement: between Washington Monument or Lincoln and Mason Neck
optionalTimelineAddOns.timelineImpact: Adds about 1h 35m before Mason Neck when including the activation and extra driving.
optionalTimelineAddOns.timelineNote: Useful Ostrich buffer if public access and pace are excellent.

operatingStops.timing: Saturday 2026-06-20 late evening, after the 20:00 EDT UTC rollover
operatingStops.name: Chesapeake and Ohio Canal NHP / Anglers parking lot
operatingStops.parking: Billy Goat sections B / Anglers parking lot
operatingStops.evidence: NPS C&O current conditions list the Anglers/Carderock corridor access points and parking as open, and Google Maps resolved Anglers parking as the clean late-arrival C&O access point. Use this as the all-states Maryland anchor when Greenbelt campground access is not available.
operatingStops.access: Use the public Anglers parking/towpath access. Count only US-0015 unless a separate verified overlap is established on site.
operatingStops.confidence: clean late-arrival Maryland single-reference opener; lower count than Greenbelt but better all-states reliability

operatingStops.timing: Fallback only: Saturday 2026-06-20 at/after 20:00 EDT if legal public access is available without relying on campground access
operatingStops.name: Greenbelt Park / Baltimore-Washington Parkway overlap
operatingStops.evidence: N2NWK email suggested a Greenbelt four-fer (US-0668 + US-7973 + US-0654 + US-4581) and described after-hours campground/parkway-style access patterns. Fresh GeoJSON confirms a practical Greenbelt/Baltimore-Washington Parkway overlap for US-0668 + US-7973; strict four-way overlap is empty, and the best US-0668/US-0654/US-7973 component misses the official Star-Spangled Banner NHT 100-foot trail buffer by about 35 feet.
operatingStops.access: Use only a legal public operating point in the confirmed overlap after the 20:00 EDT UTC rollover. Sweetgum is not the decision point for the overlap; campground or parkway-style access needs on-site judgment.
operatingStops.confidence: higher-count fallback, but not the selected late-night all-states anchor without a campsite

operatingStops.timing: Optional Saturday-local stop only if arrival is early enough for a real activation before the 18:00 EDT gate closure; not available for Sunday UTC rollover
operatingStops.name: Fort McHenry National Monument
operatingStops.evidence: Repeated imported top-activator records show US-0928 + US-4581 + US-4567 + US-4582, but official NPS access hours rule out an at/after-20:00 Sunday UTC activation.
operatingStops.confidence: strong n-fer evidence before closing; skip if Fox Point cannot be left by about 16:00 for a useful activation window

operatingStops.timing: Saturday late from the Columbia Island Marina side if timing works, or Sunday morning as the primary jackpot after the C&O Maryland opener

operatingStops.parking: Right side of the Columbia Island Marina / Lady Bird Johnson Park parking area
operatingStops.evidence: CWops/W8WZ article gives reference list; W3LAM email corroborates the right-side parking-area/LBJ operation and says vehicle, wires, stakes, tripods, and larger portable setups have worked there. W3LAM also notes entrance signs may say Lady Bird Johnson Park and Columbia Island Marina.
operatingStops.confidence: high after local activator feedback; bring headphones for DCA/Pentagon-area noise

operatingStops.confidence: practical default Virginia finish; use Mustang Equestrian Trailhead per local advice

operatingStops.confidence: imported K4NYM n-fer record; exact public position needs verification

openVerification: Use C&O Canal / Anglers as the selected late-night Maryland anchor for all-states. Count US-0015 only unless a separate verified overlap is established.
Confirm the Lady Bird Johnson Park / Columbia Island Marina right-side parking-area position in the field, but local W3LAM feedback supports all seven references there.
Do not rely on Fort McHenry for Sunday UTC: official NPS summer weekend gate closure is 18:00. Treat Fort only as optional Saturday-local if arriving before last entry.
Greenbelt/BW Parkway remains a higher-count fallback only with legal public access and a defensible exact operating point; without a campsite, do not use it as the all-states anchor.
Use Meadowood’s Mustang Equestrian Trailhead as the default Virginia finish unless the day is running well enough to justify Mason Neck.
Confirm Mason Neck NWR operating restrictions and closing/dusk times before relying on it as an add-on.
Ford’s Theatre is the first practical downtown buffer if Jefferson/President’s Park/WWI pacing or access slips.
Jefferson and Ford resolved as point GeoJSON rather than full polygons; use POTA/NPS context and on-site signage before treating the point as the whole activatable area.

expandedMallInventory.summary: DC cache has 32 references total. For the revised all-states-first rove, the practical inventory is the C&O Canal Maryland opener, the LBJ trail cluster, and the expanded Mall/downtown references needed to replace the lost Greenbelt entity; Fort McHenry is optional before 18:00 only.

expandedMallRoute.name: C&O + LBJ + expanded Mall route
expandedMallRoute.strategy: Use C&O Canal at Anglers as the clean late-night Maryland opener, then LBJ as the jackpot and the expanded Mall sequence through Jefferson, President’s Park, and WWI. C&O plus LBJ reaches 8 unique references before the Mall; Cheetah is reached at Vietnam and the route reaches 20 at WWI if all stops hold.

mapsChecks.to: Dwight D. Eisenhower Memorial via expanded Mall walk

recommendedOrder: C&O Canal / Anglers parking lot after 20:00 EDT Saturday
Optional late: LBJ / Columbia Island Marina right-side parking area if timing still works
Exact LBJ 7-fer operating area if not done Saturday night
Ford’s Theatre NHS buffer if needed
Return to Foggy Bottom hotel, then drive to Meadowood

countMilestones.label: After Jefferson starts the Mall overlay

expandedMallRoute.caution: C&O/Anglers is selected because all-states credit is higher priority than Greenbelt’s extra entity. Greenbelt remains a fallback only with legal access; Ford’s Theatre is the first downtown buffer if a Mall stop slips.

routeOptimization.summary: Revised after the late-arrival all-states decision: C&O Canal at Anglers is the clean Maryland opener, LBJ is the high-confidence jackpot, Cheetah is reached at Vietnam, the expanded Mall route reaches 20 at WWI, and Meadowood remains the practical Virginia finish.
constraints: The Tesla stays parked at the Foggy Bottom hotel during the DC rove.
Prefer walking when the time penalty is modest and it avoids another rideshare.
Use rideshare for the hotel -> LBJ and LBJ -> Mall jumps; those crossings are not worth walking during a count-focused rove.
Return to the hotel before the Virginia finish, because the southbound car move starts there.

recommendedCoreOrder.label: LBJ / Columbia Island
recommendedCoreOrder.movement: late Saturday if possible, otherwise Sunday morning

recommendedCoreOrder.label: Constitution -> WWII -> Washington -> President’s Park -> WWI

addOnFindings.label: Theodore Roosevelt Island
addOnFindings.finding: Only do it before LBJ if chasing 20+. Walking hotel -> TR -> LBJ is about 88 minutes; rideshare movement is about 13 minutes before activation time.

addOnFindings.label: LBJ to Jefferson
addOnFindings.finding: Treat this as a rideshare jump. Driving/rideshare is about 6 minutes and sets up the expanded Mall walk needed after the C&O opener.

addOnFindings.label: Return to hotel
addOnFindings.finding: After the WWI downtown finish, use rideshare back to Foggy Bottom if protecting the Meadowood Virginia stop; walking is too expensive late in the day.

addOnFindings.label: East Mall expansion
addOnFindings.finding: If you push to Eisenhower, rideshare back to the hotel is about 11 minutes; walking back is about 54 minutes and should not be the default.

addOnFindings.label: Mason Neck
addOnFindings.finding: Clean 2-fer stretch if the day is running well. Local advice says Mason Neck/Hartwell require separate positions, Hartwell has stricter setup limits, and Mason Neck State Park adds time plus an entry fee.

addOnFindings.label: Meadowood
addOnFindings.finding: Use as the practical Virginia finish. Local advice recommends the Mustang Equestrian Trailhead as the best reliable radio spot with free parking; Washington Monument -> Meadowood -> Fredericksburg is about 1h16 driving.

addOnFindings.label: Fort McHenry Saturday branch
addOnFindings.finding: Fort is still pre-18:00 only. With the late-arrival adjustment, skip it and protect the C&O / Anglers Maryland opener instead.
